name: scheduler-plugin
description: Schedule recurring prompts, dispatch them to chosen agents, track every fire and run. Week, month, and list views, seven MCP tools.
author: Plekt

tools.name: list_jobs
tools.description: List scheduled jobs with optional filters. Returns jobs ordered by id DESC plus the unfiltered total count.

enabled_only.description: If true, only return enabled jobs.

agent_id.description: Filter jobs by their assignee agent id.

tools.name: get_job
tools.description: Fetch one job by id together with its 10 most recent runs.

tools.name: create_job
tools.description: Create a new scheduled job. Validates the cron expression, computes the first fire time, and inserts the row. Emits scheduler.job.created.

prompt.description: Prompt the assignee agent will receive when the job fires.

agent_id.description: Identifier of the agent that will execute the prompt.

timezone.description: IANA timezone for the cron expression. Defaults to Europe/Berlin.

task_id.description: Optional task id to link this job to.

enabled.description: Whether the job is active. Defaults to true.

tools.name: update_job
tools.description: Partial-update an existing job. Any field omitted is left unchanged. Pass task_id=-1 to clear the task linkage. If cron_expr or timezone change, the next_fire_at is recomputed automatically. Emits scheduler.job.updated.

task_id.description: Pass -1 to clear the task linkage.

tools.name: delete_job
tools.description: Delete a job and all of its run history. Emits scheduler.job.deleted.

tools.name: trigger_job_now
tools.description: Queue an immediate firing of a job, bypassing its enabled flag and schedule. Emits scheduler.trigger.requested for the engine to pick up.

tools.name: validate_cron
tools.description: Validate a 5-field cron expression in the given IANA timezone and return up to five upcoming fire times. Read-only: does not create a job. Used by the New Job and Edit Job dialogs to preview the schedule.

timezone.description: IANA timezone (e.g. Europe/Berlin). Empty string means UTC.
